obligations.reason: JTAG unlock signal (jtag_unlock) is distributed to reglk_wrapper and may bypass lock mechanisms that protect peripheral registers. Need to verify that jtag_unlock does not override the register lock bits (reglk_ctrl) without explicit authenticated debug session.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug interface (jtag_unlock signal)
obligations.operation: bypass lock or force unlock state
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock must not set reglk_ctrl outputs to unlocked state unless a valid authentication hash (jtag_hash) has been received and the debug session is authorized; lock bits must stay asserted when jtag_unlock is not present or not authenticated.
obligations.candidate_path: jtag_unlock -> reglk_wrapper -> reglk_ctrl_o -> locks in acct_wrapper, dma_wrapper, pkt_wrapper
state_conditions_to_check: value of jtag_unlock during normal operation
generation condition of jtag_unlock from jtag_hash
how reglk_wrapper uses jtag_unlock to drive reglk_ctrl_o
lock register reset values (should be locked by default)
required_evidence_slots: RTL source for jtag_unlock generation
RTL of reglk_wrapper showing jtag_unlock effect on reglk_ctrl_o
lock registers logic in dma_wrapper, acct_wrapper, pkt_wrapper
obligations.uncertainty: Whether jtag_unlock is solely based on a hardcoded hash or correct authentication is unclear from static facts; need to confirm the authentication mechanism.

obligations.reason: Test mode input (testmode_i) may cause the chip to enter a state where normal access controls (privilege level, lock bits) are bypassed. Check that testmode_i does not disable the lock mechanism or allow direct read/write to protected registers without privilege checks.
obligations.subject: test interface (testmode_i)
obligations.object: all protected registers (e.g., DMA config, lock registers, secret key storage)
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i must not decouple write-enable or read-enable signals from priv_lvl_i, acc_ctrl, reglk_ctrl, or we_flags; test mode should only be used for DFT, not for security-sensitive register access.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i -> internal test logic -> bypass muxes in acct_wrapper, dma_wrapper, pkt_wrapper, reglk_wrapper
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of testmode_i
whether testmode_i affects the value of acc_ctrl_c array (used for ROM access)
whether we_flags are gated by testmode_i
whether testmode_i overrides reglk_ctrl or core_lock_reg
required_evidence_slots: RTL showing testmode_i usage in riscv_peripherals and all wrapper modules
comparison of access control logic with and without testmode_i asserted
obligations.uncertainty: The exact usage of testmode_i is not detailed in the fact layer; it might be used only for DFT but needs verification that it is not connected to security-critical paths.

obligations.reason: The debug module slave interface (dm_slave) can initiate AXI reads/writes. If its address decode includes registers that control locks (reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl) or directly exposed secrets, an external debugger could override protections without unlocking.
obligations.subject: debug master (dm_slave via debug module)
obligations.operation: write to lock control or access control registers, or read secret state
obligations.expected_guard: Any access to these control registers from dm_slave must be gated by the same lock/protection mechanism (e.g., jtag_unlock, debug authorization) or be entirely disabled; the dm_slave address space should not overlap with security-sensitive registers.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_slave_req -> dm_slave_addr -> AXI decode -> peripheral wrapper -> register write
state_conditions_to_check: address decode mapping of dm_slave in riscv_peripherals
whether dm_slave writes can reach acct_wrapper or reglk_wrapper
whether dm_slave reads can return pkey_loc or fuse data
whether debug_mode_i or jtag_unlock gates dm_slave accesses
required_evidence_slots: RTL of debug module connection (dm_slave signals)
address decode logic in riscv_peripherals for debug interface
sensitive register paths in wrapper modules
files_or_modules_to_inspect: riscv_peripherals (debug module instance and AXI connections)
obligations.uncertainty: The actual address map for debug slave is not provided; the obligation assumes that the debug interface can access peripheral registers. Need to verify from RTL whether such a path exists.

obligations.reason: The pkt_wrapper contains a 64-bit signal pkey_loc that likely holds a cryptographic key. It may be exposed through the AXI read path if the lock bits (reglk_ctrl) do not gate the read data. Need to verify that pkey_loc cannot be read without proper authorization.
obligations.subject: CPU AXI read or debug read (via pkt_wrapper AXI interface)
obligations.expected_guard: Access to pkey_loc via the `rdata` output of pkt_wrapper must be conditioned on reglk_ctrl being unlocked (or acct_ctrl allowing read) and must not be readable when the wrapper is locked.
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper `address` decode -> en and we logic -> rdata mux that includes pkey_loc
state_conditions_to_check: whether rdata output can carry pkey_loc
whether read access is gated by reglk_ctrl bits
whether we_flag or acct_ctrl affect read access
whether pkey_loc is constant or updated from fuse_rdata
required_evidence_slots: RTL of pkt_wrapper showing rdata generation
connection between pkey_loc and AXI read path
lock logic using reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i
obligations.uncertainty: pkey_loc may be an internal key that should never be read; the RTL must be inspected to see if it is used in a comparator only or actually muxed onto the read bus.

obligations.reason: Fuse data (fuse_rdata_i) enters pkt_wrapper and may represent non-volatile secrets. The debug path could potentially access this data via the AXI interface or through internal state if not properly isolated.
obligations.subject: debug master or test interface
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse read data should only be accessible when a specific unlock sequence (triggered via fuse_req_o) is active and must not be exposed through general AXI reads or debug reads.
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_rdata_i -> pkt_wrapper -> rdata port -> AXI response or debug path
state_conditions_to_check: whether fuse_rdata is latched internally and readable
whether the read path includes a lock check
whether debug/test can trigger a fuse read
required_evidence_slots: RTL of pkt_wrapper fuse interface
how fuse_rdata is used in the read mux
any direct connection to debug signal
obligations.uncertainty: Fuse data is likely secret; assuming it is the source of pkey_loc, but needs confirmation.

obligations.reason: The jtag_unlock signal is generated from jtag_hash (256-bit). If the comparison logic is weak (e.g., hardcoded compare, no authentication state machine) or can be bypassed by testmode_i or reset, an attacker can drive it high.
obligations.subject: JTAG input (jtag_hash)
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock must only be asserted after a proper authentication protocol; it should not be forced high by testmode_i, debug_mode_i, or any reset condition.
state_conditions_to_check: comparator logic (is a fixed expected value used?)
effect of testmode_i on jtag_unlock
effect of debug_mode_i on jtag_unlock
reset value of jtag_unlock
required_evidence_slots: RTL of riscv_peripherals showing jtag_unlock generation
comparator definition
obligations.uncertainty: If the expected hash is a known constant or selectable via fuses, security may be weak; needs investigation.

obligations.reason: Lock registers such as core_lock_reg in DMA and the reglk_ctrl registers in acct_wrapper, dma_wrapper, etc., must default to a locked state (1 = locked, or set appropriately) after reset. If they default to unlocked, a transient unlocked window could allow unauthorised configuration writes.
obligations.subject: reset logic
obligations.object: core_lock_reg, reglk_ctrl registers, any lock bits
obligations.expected_guard: All lock registers must reset to a value that enforces protection (e.g., core_lock_reg=1, reglk_ctrl bits set to locked).
obligations.candidate_path: rst_ni -> internal reset logic -> lock register flops
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of core_lock_reg in dma_wrapper
reset value of reglk_ctrl in reglk_wrapper and acct_wrapper
any asynchronous set/clear during reset
required_evidence_slots: RTL of dma_wrapper lock register instantiation
RTL of reglk_wrapper reset state
RTL of acct_wrapper lock register reset
obligations.uncertainty: The exact lock behavior (active-high or active-low) is not specified; default state must be assumed as 'locked'.

obligations.reason: The write-enable flag (we_flag) is input to each wrapper and likely derived from CPU privilege mode and memory address decode. If debug_mode_i overrides we_flag and forces write enable, an external debugger could write to locked registers.
obligations.subject: debug mode (debug_mode_i)
obligations.operation: override we_flag to force write enable
obligations.object: peripheral register writes (e.g., DMA config, lock registers)
obligations.expected_guard: debug_mode_i must not affect we_flag; we_flag should always reflect the intended CPU privilege check and not be bypassed.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i -> logic in riscv_peripherals or acct_wrapper that modifies we_flag behavior
state_conditions_to_check: how we_flag is generated (fact layer shows we_flag_* inputs to riscv_peripherals, then passed to wrappers)
whether debug_mode_i alters the connectivity of we_flag to the AXI write enable
whether we_flag is used inside wrapper modules for gating writes
required_evidence_slots: RTL of riscv_peripherals showing we_flag routing
RTL of each wrapper showing how we_flag gates write operations
obligations.uncertainty: The exact role of we_flag is not described; assumption is that it is the write strobe from the CPU and should be honored.

obligations.reason: The DMA engine contains a core_lock_reg meant to lock the DMA configuration registers. After the lock is set, writes to start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_*, dest_addr_* should be ignored until an unlock sequence. Verify that the lock is effective on all write paths, including during an active DMA transfer, and cannot be bypassed by setting start_reg before locking.
obligations.subject: any bus master (CPU or debug) writing to DMA registers
obligations.operation: write to DMA configuration registers while lock is set
obligations.object: DMA configuration registers (start_reg, length_reg, source/dest addr regs, valid)
obligations.expected_guard: If core_lock_reg is asserted, writes to the listed registers must be blocked; the lock state must be checked at each write clock edge. Also ensure that a write to core_lock_reg itself is secure (cannot be undone without proper key).
obligations.candidate_path: AXI write -> dma_wrapper address decode -> we condition uses core_lock_reg
state_conditions_to_check: logic for register write-enable that includes core_lock_reg check
whether core_lock_reg can be cleared by a write that bypasses the lock (e.g., direct debug write)
behavior when start_reg is written before lock is set (TOCTOU)
required_evidence_slots: RTL of dma_wrapper showing register write enables
lock check combinational path
obligations.uncertainty: The lock model (sticky until reset or unlock sequence) is unknown; must confirm.

obligations.reason: The boot ROM access is gated by priv_lvl_i via acc_ctrl_c array (`rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]`). If debug_mode_i can elevate priv_lvl_i or override the check, an external debugger could read or even modify the boot ROM (if writeable) bypassing privilege.
obligations.subject: debugger (via debug_mode_i) or testmode_i
obligations.operation: bypass privilege check on boot ROM access
obligations.expected_guard: Debug mode must not change priv_lvl_i or bypass the acc_ctrl_c check; boot ROM address space must remain protected.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i -> priv_lvl_i mux or override in riscv_peripherals -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]
state_conditions_to_check: how priv_lvl_i is generated (from CPU status?)
effect of debug_mode_i on priv_lvl_i
whether debug request (debug_req) can target boot ROM addresses
whether testmode_i affects acc_ctrl_c
required_evidence_slots: RTL of riscv_peripherals showing priv_lvl_i source and influence
access control logic for boot ROM
obligations.uncertainty: ROM is likely read-only; the main concern is unauthorized read exposure from debugger, which might be acceptable if debug is authenticated. The obligation is to check that debug does not bypass the privilege check.
